message:
fix(core): harden symbolic executor against opaque commands, tilde paths, and input redirects

The symbolic executor had several gaps that allowed dangerous commands
to pass safety analysis:

Opaque execution binaries:
- Remove python, python3, ruby, node, deno, perl, awk, sed, curl,
  wget, http, ssh, scp, rsync, docker, podman, kubectl, helm from
  SAFE_BINARIES — their argv cannot be reliably safety-analyzed
  (e.g., python -c "import os; os.remove('/etc/passwd')")
- Create OPAQUE_EXECUTION_BINARIES list; these are now flagged as
  ViolationCategory::Unparseable with an explanatory message

Path argument analysis refinement:
- Create PATH_OPERAND_SAFE_BINARIES (cat, head, tail, ls, stat, etc.)
  whose non-flag operands are reliably path-like. These get path
  traversal checking even though they are in the safe set
- Exclude pattern/script-driven tools (grep, sed, awk) from path
  checking to avoid false positives on literals like '/etc/' in
  search patterns

Tilde expansion:
- Add TILDE_SENTINEL_ROOT constant and handle ~-prefixed paths in
  resolve_path() by mapping them under a synthetic absolute root,
  ensuring they fall outside any non-root CWD boundary
- Prevents "cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa" from being classified as safe

Input redirect checking:
- Extend check_redirects to flag input redirects (<) outside CWD as
  PathTraversal, not just output redirects (>). Reading sensitive
  files via "grep secret < /etc/shadow" is also a boundary violation

Tests (7 new):
- safe_cat_within_cwd, unsafe_input_redirect_outside_cwd,
  unsafe_cat_absolute_outside_cwd, unsafe_tilde_path_outside_cwd,
  unsafe_python_inline_code_is_opaque, unsafe_ssh_remote_command_is_opaque,
  safe_grep_absolute_like_pattern_literal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
